title: Combustion and Flame 137 (2004) 148–162
abstract:
Combustion of stationary JP8 fuel droplets mixed with additives is examined in a low gravity environment to
reduce the inﬂuence of convection and promote spherical droplet ﬂames. Both qualitative information on ﬂame
structure and sooting tendencies and quantitative information on evaporation rates and ﬂame and soot shell di-
ameters are reported. The additives were hexanol (C6H14O) and a commercial blend of a detergent/dispersant
termed “+100” that is widely used as an additive in military trans
